Pathway_Genes.Breeding_Application_Value: Key target for engineering soybean isoflavonoid composition, glycitein content, and Phytophthora sojae resistance; precise tuning rather than simple overexpression is suggested for disease-resilient cultivars.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes A-ring 6-hydroxylation of daidzein to form 6-hydroxydaidzein, establishing the predominant route to glycitein in soybean; also hydroxylates formononetin and biochanin A in vitro.
Pathway_Genes.Chromosome_Position: Chr11 near 8,259,672 bp lead SNP; exact gene coordinates NA
Pathway_Genes.Cofactor_or_Coenzyme_Requirement: Cytochrome P450 monooxygenase; requires NADPH regenerating system in microsomal assay; Mn2+ and Ca2+ enhanced activity, whereas Fe2+, Fe3+, and Cu2+ inhibited activity; heme-dependent P450 inferred.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Required for glycitein-type isoflavonoid accumulation and for balanced defense against P. sojae; knockout nearly abolishes glycitein-type isoflavonoids, whereas overexpression increases 6-hydroxydaidzein/glycitein-related products but can reduce glyceollin I and increase pathogen susceptibility.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: mGWAS; hairy-root overexpression metabolite profiling; yeast substrate-feeding assay; microsomal enzyme assay and kinetics; CRISPR-Cas9 knockout; stable-isotope tracing with daidzein-d6; transgenic overexpression in soybean; pathogen infection assay; chemical complementation with glycitein.
Pathway_Genes.Developmental_or_Physiological_Context: Seed isoflavonoid variation in a natural population; hypocotyls of 2-d-old germinating seedlings; mature roots; 3-d-old etiolated seedlings during P. sojae infection.
Pathway_Genes.Evidence_for_Enzymatic_Function: Yeast expressing GmIF6H1 converted daidzein specifically to 6-hydroxydaidzein; microsomal assays established kinetic parameters; isotope tracing in planta produced labeled glycitein-d5 derivatives from daidzein-d6 in wild type and OE but not mutant.
Pathway_Genes.Evidence_for_Pathway_Position: gmif6h1 mutants lacked glycitein-type isoflavonoids and accumulated more daidzin/malonyldaidzin; OE accumulated 6-hydroxydaidzein derivatives; 6-hydroxyliquiritigenin was not detected; daidzein-d6 feeding generated labeled 6-hydroxydaidzein/glycitein derivatives.
Pathway_Genes.Field_Trials: Natural population grown in Beijing in 2013 and in Beijing, Heilongjiang, and Henan in 2014 for seed isoflavonoid phenotyping; no dedicated disease-resistance field trial reported.
Pathway_Genes.Gene_Discovery_or_Cloning_Method: Metabolic GWAS followed by candidate-gene prioritization within qMGly_11 region and functional validation.
Pathway_Genes.Genetic_Population: 727-accession soybean natural population for metabolite profiling/GWAS; 528-accession representative population for haplotype analysis; CRISPR mutants and overexpression lines in Williams 82; hairy roots in Kefeng-1.
Pathway_Genes.Key_Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: P. sojae infection; daidzein-d6 feeding; germination stage; greenhouse and multi-location field growth for seed profiling.
Pathway_Genes.Key_Variant_Site: +742 coding SNP causing Ala248Thr (Hap1 A248 vs Hap2 T248 terminology in paper: Hap1 Ala248, Hap2 Thr248)
Pathway_Genes.Metabolite_Assay_Method: LC-qToF-MS; LC-MS; LC-QQQ-MS/MS; metabolite profiling during infection and isotope labeling.
Pathway_Genes.Notes_or_Other_Important_Info: Originally referred to as GmCYP76F17/F6H4 candidate; renamed GmIF6H1 in this study. Although enzyme shows higher in vitro activity with formononetin and biochanin A, these substrates were absent under tested soybean hypocotyl conditions, supporting daidzein as bona fide in planta substrate. Both loss and overexpression increase susceptibility, indicating optimal flux balance is critical.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Resequencing/GWAS SNP dataset from previous study including 53 soja accessions; metabolomics datasets S1-S3; transcriptome profiling comparing GmIF6H1OE-1 and W82 during infection accession NA.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Phenotypic_Effects: No significant effect on nodulation number or size; overexpression altered afromosin/formononetin-type isoflavonoid balance during infection.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Overexpression increases susceptibility to P. sojae by diverting flux away from glyceollin biosynthesis; simple elevation of glycitein is detrimental to defense balance.
Pathway_Genes.Quantitative_Phenotypic_Alterations: Knockout nearly abolished glycitein-type isoflavonoids in 2-d hypocotyls and increased daidzin/malonyldaidzin by ~1.5-fold; OE accumulated glycosylated/malonylated 6-hydroxydaidzein ~2- to 3-fold; Hap2 showed ~5-fold higher catalytic efficiency than Hap1 (1.48 ± 0.17 vs 0.28 ± 0.042 s^-1 M^-1); glyceollin I in OE during infection 5.90 vs 9.36 μmol g^-1 DW in W82 at 2 dpi.
Pathway_Genes.Target_Product_Level_or_Property: Content/accumulation and pathway flux partitioning relative to glyceollins

Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Isoflavonoid biosynthesis in Medicago; likely afromosin/irisolidone-related 6-hydroxylation branch
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_Application_Value: Potential metabolic engineering target for tailoring 6-methoxylated isoflavonoids in legumes; direct breeding relevance not assessed in this paper.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Hydroxylates formononetin to 6-hydroxyformononetin and biochanin A to a putative hydroxylated product annotated here as 6-hydroxybiochanin A.
Pathway_Genes.Cofactor_or_Coenzyme_Requirement: Cytochrome P450 monooxygenase; NADPH-dependent microsomal/yeast P450 system inferred from assay platform.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Confers 6-hydroxylation activity toward methylated isoflavones in heterologous yeast assays, supporting conservation of legume CYP76F isoflavone hydroxylase function.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Yeast substrate-feeding assay with LC-MS product detection and comparative substrate-specificity analysis.
Pathway_Genes.Developmental_or_Physiological_Context: Heterologous biochemical characterization in yeast; no in planta developmental context tested in this paper.
Pathway_Genes.Enzyme_Name: Cytochrome P450 CYP76F family isoflavone 6-hydroxylase-like enzyme
Pathway_Genes.Evidence_for_Enzymatic_Function: Yeast assays detected 6-hydroxyformononetin from formononetin and a hydroxylated biochanin A product.
Pathway_Genes.Evidence_for_Pathway_Position: Known Medicago metabolite context and direct hydroxylated product formation place the enzyme in downstream tailoring of isoflavones.
Pathway_Genes.Gene_Discovery_or_Cloning_Method: Homology/phylogenetic selection of closest GmIF6H1 homolog followed by heterologous functional testing.
Pathway_Genes.Key_Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Yeast feeding with formononetin and biochanin A.
Pathway_Genes.Notes_or_Other_Important_Info: Study infers this enzyme may underlie biosynthesis of afromosin and irisolidone in Medicago truncatula; exact hydroxyl position on biochanin A-derived product still requires confirmation.
Pathway_Genes.Quantitative_Phenotypic_Alterations: Displayed activity comparable to GmIF6H1 and was used as 100% reference for formononetin in relative activity assays.

Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Isoflavonoid biosynthesis candidate homolog; tested for isoflavone 6-hydroxylation
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: No catalytic activity detected toward tested substrates in yeast assay.
Pathway_Genes.Cofactor_or_Coenzyme_Requirement: Cytochrome P450 monooxygenase system inferred; no activity observed.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: No detectable isoflavone 6-hydroxylase activity in heterologous assay.
Pathway_Genes.Enzyme_Name: CYP76F homolog with no detected activity under tested conditions
Pathway_Genes.Evidence_for_Enzymatic_Function: No products detected in yeast feeding assays.
Pathway_Genes.Gene_Discovery_or_Cloning_Method: Homology-based candidate selection and heterologous testing.
Pathway_Genes.Notes_or_Other_Important_Info: Lotus japonicus lacks reported 6-hydroxyisoflavonoids consistent with absence of detected activity for this homolog.
Pathway_Genes.Quantitative_Phenotypic_Alterations: No detectable activity in yeast assay.

Pathway_Genes.Cofactor_or_Coenzyme_Requirement: Cytochrome P450 monooxygenase system inferred; no activity observed.

Regulation_Genes.Breeding_Application_Value: No support from this paper for use in breeding for isoflavonoid traits.
Regulation_Genes.Chromosome_Position: Chr11 candidate interval around lead SNP; exact coordinates NA
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Altering expression did not impact isoflavonoid content in the soybean hairy root system.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Candidate-gene testing in hairy roots with expression alteration and LC-MS isoflavonoid profiling.
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Not supported; excluded from further investigation.
Regulation_Genes.Developmental_or_Physiological_Context: Soybean hairy root assay in Kefeng-1.
Regulation_Genes.Gene_Discovery_or_Cloning_Method: Candidate transcription factor within 200-kb GWAS interval around glycitein-associated SNP.
Regulation_Genes.Genetic_Population: Soybean natural population GWAS candidate interval; functional test in Kefeng-1 hairy roots.
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Not established for isoflavonoid metabolism in this paper.
Regulation_Genes.Metabolite_Assay_Method: LC-MS/LC-qToF-MS isoflavonoid profiling in hairy roots.
Regulation_Genes.Notes_or_Other_Important_Info: Although located within the candidate interval, experimental manipulation showed no detectable effect on isoflavonoid accumulation, so the gene was excluded as causal.
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: GWAS/metabolomics candidate region data; accession NA
Regulation_Genes.Quantitative_Phenotypic_Alterations: No significant impact on isoflavonoid content reported.

Regulation_Genes.Breeding_Application_Value: No support from this paper for use in breeding for isoflavonoid traits.
Regulation_Genes.Chromosome_Position: Chr11 candidate interval around lead SNP; exact coordinates NA
Regulation_Genes.Condition_Dependence_of_Regulation: Expression nearly undetectable in tested tissues.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: No evidence supporting a role in isoflavonoid variation in this study; excluded from further analysis.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Expression analysis across tissues/candidate evaluation; gene not pursued because transcript was nearly undetectable.
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Not supported; excluded from further investigation.
Regulation_Genes.Developmental_or_Physiological_Context: Initial candidate evaluation in soybean tissues.
Regulation_Genes.Gene_Discovery_or_Cloning_Method: Candidate transcription factor within 200-kb GWAS interval around glycitein-associated SNP.
Regulation_Genes.Genetic_Population: Soybean natural population GWAS candidate interval.
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Not established for isoflavonoid metabolism in this paper.
Regulation_Genes.Notes_or_Other_Important_Info: GmMYB1 expression was nearly undetectable in all tissues examined and the gene was excluded from further investigation.
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: GWAS/metabolomics candidate region data; accession NA
Regulation_Genes.Tissue_or_Organ_of_Metabolite_Assessment: Tissues surveyed for expression; exact set NA

Title: A balance between glycitein and glyceollins governed by isoflavone 6-hydroxylase confers soybean resistance to Phytophthora sojae
